findings.title: Worker Grafana panel was edited in the generated JSON instead of its generator input
findings.why: This dashboard change introduces a second source of truth for the worker panel layout. `tidb_worker.json` is regenerated from `tidb_with_keyspace_name.json`, but the PR only edits the generated worker file, so the next regeneration will silently drop the new row title and panel.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: The committed worker dashboard will drift from its checked-in generator input, and any later `generate_json.sh` run will overwrite this panel addition. That makes the change non-reviewable as a lasting source update and creates easy regressions for future dashboard edits.
findings.evidence: `pkg/metrics/nextgengrafana/generate_json.sh:27-31` rebuilds `tidb_worker.json` from `tidb_with_keyspace_name.json`. The PR adds `Write/Ingest API Duration` and renames the row to `Global Sort & Ingest` only in `pkg/metrics/nextgengrafana/tidb_worker.json:25313-25408`, while `pkg/metrics/nextgengrafana/tidb_with_keyspace_name.json:26194-26208` still has only `Active Parallel Upload Worker Count` under the old `Global Sort` row.
findings.change_request: Move this dashboard change into the maintained generator input for the worker dashboard, then regenerate `tidb_worker.json` from that source so the committed JSON and its upstream definition stay in sync.
